items.name: JobManager.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: JobManager.jobs
items.owner: JobManager, accessed by any goroutine calling Start/Stop/Status/Output or the internal wait goroutine
items.protection: sync.RWMutex (jm.mu): writes via mu.Lock(), reads via mu.RLock() or mu.Lock()
items.comment_claim: Line 16 comment: 'using mutex to protect the job lifecycles state'
items.status_reason: Every access to jm.jobs is covered: write at line 56 under mu.Lock(); reads at line 69 under mu.RLock(), line 97 under mu.Lock(), and line 119 under mu.RLock(); wait() (line 80) holds mu.Lock() but operates only on the already-dereferenced *Job pointer, never indexing jm.jobs directly.

items.name: OutputBuffer.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.cond
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.buf
items.owner: OutputBuffer, shared across all readers via ob pointer
items.protection: sync.Mutex (ob.mu) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: Write docstring says it 'appends chunk to buffer'; Read docstring says 'blocks until data is available' — both imply mutex-guarded access but do not explicitly state it
items.status_reason: Written only at line 38 inside ob.mu.Lock(); read at lines 57 and 63 both inside obr.ob.mu.Lock() — same mutex instance, no unguarded access found.

items.name: OutputBuffer.done
items.owner: OutputBuffer, written by OutputBuffer.Close(), read by readers
items.protection: sync.Mutex (ob.mu) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: OutputBuffer.Close() comment says 'marks buffer done and wakes readers'; OutputBufferReader.Read comment says 'buffer is closed + drained' — intent matches code but comments do not explicitly claim mutex protection
items.status_reason: Written at line 79 inside ob.mu.Lock(); read at lines 57 and 67 both inside obr.ob.mu.Lock() — same mutex, all sites covered.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.ob
items.protection: immutable after construction — write-once at line 29 in NewReader(), never reassigned
items.status_reason: Field is assigned once at line 29 (NewReader) before the reader is shared; all subsequent uses are reads of a stable pointer, so no synchronisation is required.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.offset
items.protection: sync.Mutex (ob.mu) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: Read docstring says 'a reader starting at offset 0 receives the full history' — implies sequential per-reader state but makes no explicit synchronisation claim
items.status_reason: Read at lines 57 and 63, written at line 64 — all three sites are inside obr.ob.mu.Lock() in Read(); no other method touches offset.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.closed
items.owner: OutputBufferReader, written by OutputBufferReader.Close(), read by Read()
items.protection: sync.Mutex (ob.mu) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: OutputBufferReader.Close() comment says 'unblocks any pending Read, which returns io.ErrClosedPipe' — describes the observable effect but does not explicitly claim mutex protection
items.status_reason: Written at line 85 inside obr.ob.mu.Lock() in OutputBufferReader.Close(); read at line 57 inside obr.ob.mu.Lock() in Read() — same mutex, all sites covered.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.drained
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism
